summary: mddriver fetches and executes remote code from anonymous paste host on import
details: The package is advertised as a 'MongoDB connection driver' but index.js is a verbatim copy of Node's built-in path module with an injected payload appended. On require/import, an IIFE decodes a base64-encoded URL (https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/C4H0M, an anonymous JSON paste service), fetches its content, and executes the returned `data.content` via a dynamically constructed `Function.constructor` (the constructor name is obfuscated by slicing 6 characters off a string literal to yield 'eval'). This is arbitrary remote code execution at import time against any consumer of the package, sourced from a mutable, unpinned, attacker-controlled host. The MongoDB framing has no basis in the shipped code and serves as a lure to attract developers searching for a database driver.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
